creator: thefloridaqueenie
caption:
Looking for things to do around Florida? Comment "Guide" and I'll send you my interactive Florida map and guide with over 1,000 spots to explore! I did the work for you so you can just hit the road without hours of research and stress!
The Ringling Museum is a must see when visiting Sarasota and you need to put it on your list of fun things to do! Pro tip: Save $25 pp and go on Mondays because you get in for free! #visitsarasota #sarasotaflorida #florida #hiddengems #ringlingbros #ringlingmuseum #floridatravel #floridatraveltips #travelhack #traveltipsandhacks #thingstodo #bucketlist2024 #daytrip

transcription.text: If you've never been to the Ringling Museum in Sarasota, Florida, this is your sign to put it on your bucket list. I recently visited, and I'm going to show you all the things you must see and how to get in for absolutely free. This property sprawls 66 acres, and you could spend a whole day here exploring, so bring your walking shoes. First visit the Cottazon, which was John and Mabel Ringling's winter home, built in the 1920s overlooking the beautiful Sarasota Bay. The home was inspired by the Venetian Gothic style that they fell in love with during their travels in Italy. This palace is awe-inspiring to see in person, but it does cause extra to tour the inside, but the outside is totally free. John and Mabel Ringling's gravesites are located on site in the secret garden. The art museum opened in the 1930s and houses thousands of pieces of art and antiquities from around the world, some 4,500 years old. Five of Peter Paul Rubin's Triumph of the Eucharist series are here, and the other two are in the Louvre. There are 21 galleries in total, and it can be overwhelming at times, but just take your time and wander through. There's also a large section dedicated to Asian art and antiquities, where you will find some of the oldest things in the museum, and also a section of contemporary art. The museum's courtyard is full of Greco-Roman statues, all under the watchful eye of a 17-foot tall bronze replica of Michelangelo's David. The courtyard is a beautiful place to take pictures and hang out. Do not skip out on exploring through the banyan trees. I can't say this enough.
